CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT GRANILLO, RHINA MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, INC. ALVA, RICARDO JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 10.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RHINA GRANILLO, MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, INC. & RICARDO ALVA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED. PLAINTIFF FILED THIS LAWSUIT ON JANUARY 4, 2013. AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED PURSUANT TO GOV. CODE 12965(B). THE RIGHT-TO-SUE NOTICE ISSUED TO PLAINTIFF IS DATED OCTOBER 29, 2009. (RJN, EXHIBIT A.) THESE CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED BECAUSE THEY WERE NOT FILED WITHIN ONE YEAR OF THE ISSUANCE OF THE RIGHT TO SUE LETTER. PAUL V. GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA (2011) 193 CAL. APP. 4TH 718, 225. AS TO THE SECOND, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE TIME-BARRED PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE 335.1. PLAINTIFF'S INJURY OCCURRED ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2009 WHEN HIS EMPLOYMENT WAS TERMINATED. (FIRST AM. COMPLAINT, 12B) TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF RELIES ON HIS PREVIOUS LAWSUIT IN THIS COURT, VELAZQUEZ V. MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL (CASE CGC-10-502272), THAT ENTIRE ACTION WAS DISMISSED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE, ON OCTOBER 9, 2012." 'A DISMISSAL "WITHOUT PREJUDICE" NECESSARILY MEANS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE FILING OF A NEW ACTION ON THE SAME ALLEGATIONS, SO LONG AS IT IS DONE WITHIN THE PERIOD OF THE APPROPRIATE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. [CITATIONS.]' [CITATIONS.]" (CARDIFF EQUITIES, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2008) 166 CAL.APP.4TH 1541, 1550.) PLAINTIFF DID NOT TIMELY FILE HIS NEW ACTION WITHIN THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PERIOD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-30-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2013 Continued To Feb-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS RHINA GRANILLO, MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, INC. & RICARDO ALVA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Edward Garcia, Jr. of the Law Offices of Bragg & Kuluva (415.273.6500) appearing for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.

This matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained, without leave to amend. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff's claims are time-barred. Plaintiff filed this lawsuit on January 4, 2013. As to the first, third and fourth causes of action, Plaintiff's claims are time-barred pursuant to Gov. Code 12965(b). The Right-to-Sue Notice issued to Plaintiff is dated October 29, 2009. (RJN, Exhibit A.) These claims are time barred because they were not filed within one year of the issuance of the right to sue letter. Paul v. Goodwill Industries of Southern California (2011) 193 Cal. App. 4th 718, 225. As to the second, fifth and sixth causes of action, Plaintiff's claims are time-barred pursuant to Civil Code 335.1. Plaintiff's injury occurred on September 17, 2009 when his employment was terminated. (First Am. Complaint, 12b) To the extent that Plaintiff relies on his previous lawsuit in this court, Velazquez v. Mission Language and Vocational School (Case CGC-10-502272), that entire action was dismissed, without prejudice, on October 9, 2012." 'A dismissal "without prejudice" necessarily means without prejudice to the filing of a new action on the same allegations, so long as it is done within the period of the appropriate statute of limitations. [Citations.]' [Citations.]" (Cardiff Equities, Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1541, 1550.) Plaintiff did not timely file his new action within the applicable statute of limitations period. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Continued To Dec-30-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS RHINA GRANILLO'S, MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, INC.'S, and RICARDO ALVA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to December 30, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 9, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
